required_skills: Design, develop, and maintain web applications
Build responsive UI using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React or similar frameworks
Develop backend services using Node.js, Python (Flask/Django), or similar technologies
Design and manage databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB)
Implement RESTful APIs and integrate third-party services
Ensure performance, scalability, and security of applications
Strong knowledge of JavaScript and modern frontend frameworks
Experience with backend frameworks and API development
Understanding of database design and query optimization
missing_skills: Design, develop, and maintain web applications
Build responsive UI using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React or similar frameworks
Develop backend services using Node.js, Python (Flask/Django), or similar technologies
Design and manage databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB)
Implement RESTful APIs and integrate third-party services
Ensure performance, scalability, and security of applications
Strong knowledge of JavaScript and modern frontend frameworks
Experience with backend frameworks and API development
Understanding of database design and query optimization

level_2.title: Technical Questions
questions: What are the basic features of Java?
How do you create a class in Java?
What is the difference between interface and abstract class?

level_3.title: Behavioral Questions
questions: Share a situation where you had to take ownership beyond your formal responsibilities to move a project forward.
Tell me about a time you had to make a difficult decision while working on Python and how the result affected your team.
Tell me about a time you had to meet a tight deadline.

questions.question: The job description emphasizes Python. Which option is the strongest interview response?
options: Claim Python is mostly handled automatically so deep understanding is unnecessary.
Describe where Python fits in the system, why it was chosen, and what constraints it solved.
Focus on unrelated tools and hope the interviewer infers knowledge of Python.
Give a memorized definition of Python without examples, tradeoffs, or project context.
